definition_en: Something bought at a lower price than usual
example: I found a real bargain at the market — a jacket for five pounds.

definition_en: A printed document showing what you bought and how much you paid
example: Keep your receipt in case you want to return the item.

definition_en: Money returned to a customer for something bought
example: She requested a refund because the dress was damaged.

definition_en: A reduction in the usual price of something
example: Students get a 10% discount at this store.

definition_en: To look at things in a shop without necessarily intending to buy
example: I was just browsing when I found something I liked.

definition_en: The place in a shop where you pay, or the process of paying online
example: There was a long queue at the checkout.

definition_en: A card that gives customers rewards or points for repeated purchases
example: I collect points on my loyalty card to get discounts.

definition_en: The sale of goods directly to consumers
example: The retail sector has been transformed by online shopping.

definition_en: A person who buys goods or services
example: Consumers are becoming more aware of sustainability.

definition_en: Something bought without planning, on a sudden desire
example: The chocolates at the checkout were an impulse buy.

definition_en: Previously owned and used by someone else
example: She buys most of her clothes second-hand.

definition_en: A written promise to repair or replace a product if it breaks
example: The laptop comes with a two-year warranty.

definition_en: A room in a shop where you try on clothes
example: The fitting rooms were all occupied.

definition_en: Buying large quantities of something, usually at a lower price
example: Bulk buying can save money if you have storage space.

definition_en: The buying and selling of goods and services over the internet
example: E-commerce grew rapidly during the pandemic.

definition_en: A label showing the price of an item
example: Always check the price tag before buying.

definition_en: No longer available because all items have been sold
example: The shoes I wanted were out of stock in my size.

definition_en: A regular payment to receive a product or service
example: She cancelled her streaming subscription to save money.

definition_en: The regular expenses of running a business such as rent and utilities
example: High overhead costs made the small shop unprofitable.

definition_en: Returning a product to get a different one in its place
example: I'd like to exchange this shirt for a larger size.

definition_en: A period when a shop sells products at reduced prices
example: The winter sale starts on the 26th of December.

definition_en: A document requesting payment for goods or services provided
example: The company sent an invoice for the consulting work.
